question_text: A car is travelling at a constant speed in a straight line on a flat road. Which statement best describes the forces acting on the car?

options.label: A
options.text: The driving force is greater than the friction force, so there is a resultant force forwards

options.label: B
options.text: The driving force is equal to the friction force, so the resultant force is zero

options.label: C
options.text: The driving force is less than the friction force, so the car is accelerating

options.label: D
options.text: There is no friction force when the car travels at constant speed

explanation: When an object moves at constant speed, the forces are balanced. The driving force must equal the friction force (air resistance and road friction) so that the resultant force is zero. Newton's first law states that an object at constant velocity stays at constant velocity if there is no resultant force.
